title: pickle.PickleBuffer: concurrent .raw() and .release() race the shared Py_buffer view (picklebuf_raw vs PyBuffer_Release, Objects/picklebufobject.c)
description: pickle.PickleBuffer wraps a Py_buffer view. On a free-threaded build, a shared PickleBuffer used concurrently races: .raw() (picklebuf_raw, Objects/picklebufobject.c:155) reads self->view while .release() (picklebuf_release:196 -> PyBuffer_Release, abstract.c:827) writes/invalidates it -> data race on the view (and .raw() may read a just-released buffer). No per-object synchronization.
sites: Objects/picklebufobject.c picklebuf_raw:155 (READ self->view to build the raw memoryview)
race_kind: shared buffer-object state: concurrent read (.raw()) vs release (.release()) of a PickleBuffer's Py_buffer with no per-object lock -> data race, and a read of a released buffer (memory-unsafe-ish).
shared_state: PicklebufObject.view (the Py_buffer) on a shared PickleBuffer
severity: low-to-moderate: a .raw() racing .release() can read a buffer being torn down (use-after-release of the view's fields). Sharing one PickleBuffer and releasing it while another thread reads it is unusual, which caps priority. Free-threaded build only.
repro_reliability: deterministic: repro.py (8 threads sharing one pickle.PickleBuffer, half .raw() half .release()) exits 66 on debug-ft-nojit-tsan; signature matches the magalu fleet exactly. Reproduced on our build.
suggested_fix: Take the PickleBuffer's per-object critical section around .raw() and .release() (and the buffer-export path), or document that a PickleBuffer must not be shared/released concurrently.
prior_art: gh api search found no PickleBuffer free-threading issue -> appears unfiled. Shared mutable-buffer-object race.
confirmed_build: 3.16_ft_debug_tsan (magalu remote); reproduced independently on our debug-ft-nojit-tsan
related: Shared mutable-object race. Surfaced in a downloaded remote fleet.
notes: NEW / appears unfiled. Low priority (unusual sharing: releasing a buffer while another thread reads it). Cataloged for dedup; fileable only if the maintainers want PickleBuffer to lock. Not proposing a filing.
